title: Forward mouse wheel events but not clicks or drags
author: Totktonada
body:
My scenario: I use tmux and I like that scrolling is forwarded inside the multiplexer. This way the scrolling feels natural in split window mode and in applications (for example, claude).

However, it is disappointing that I can't use usual copy-and-paste method: press+move to select a text and a middle-mouse click to paste it. I need to hold Shift if I'm in tmux. It breaks the habit that just works in all the other cases (including terminals where I've no tmux started, local ones).

My proposal is to provide ability to configure forwarding of the scrolling events, but handle mouse press (and move?) events on the terminal side.

Should I provide any other details?

title: Cannot use Esc as meta key
author: delreluca
body:
On other terminals (e.g., Terminal.app, xfce4-terminal, mate-terminal, …) I can press <kbd>esc</kbd>+another key to emulate <kbd>meta</kbd>+that key.

On alacritty it's possible to emulate <kbd>meta</kbd> with <kbd>alt</kbd>/<kbd>option</kbd> (the latter depending on `window.option_as_alt`), but a raw <kbd>esc</kbd> is not accepted.

Repro: I type <kbd>esc</kbd>+<kbd>s</kbd> in fish which is supposed to recall the last command with `sudo`/`doas` prepended.

In alacritty it just types `s`.

Tested on:
- macOS 15.7.7 / alacritty 0.17.0 / fish 4.8.0 (both via MacPorts 2.12.5)
- Alpine Linux 3.24.1 / alacritty 0.17.0 / fish 4.6.0 (both via apk repos alpine 3.24 main and community)

title: New window doesn't get keyboard focus in Cinnamon (missing _NET_WM_USER_TIME)
author: sporteka2
body:
## Environment
- Alacritty 0.13.2
- Ubuntu 24.04
- Cinnamon (Mutter/Muffin) on X11
- `focus-mode: click`, `focus-new-windows: strict`

## Problem
When launching Alacritty from the application menu (or via `gtk-launch`), the new window appears but does **not** receive keyboard focus. The user must click inside the terminal before typing. Other applications (Firefox, Nemo, GNOME Terminal) do get focus automatically in the same setup.

## Investigation
Diagnosed with `xprop`:

- Alacritty window has **no** `_NET_WM_USER_TIME` atom set
- Firefox and other GTK-based terminals **do** set this atom and get focus correctly
- Adding a `.desktop` file with `StartupNotify=true` + `StartupWMClass=Alacritty` did **not** help

### Root cause (likely)
Alacritty uses `winit` for window management. Based on the absence of `_NET_WM_USER_TIME` on the window, it appears that `winit` does not set this X11 property when creating the window. Cinnamon/Mutter relies on `_NET_WM_USER_TIME` to decide whether to auto-focus a new window.

### Note
I have not verified whether this is a `winit` limitation or an Alacritty-specific issue. It is also possible that `winit` sets `_NET_WM_USER_TIME` but Alacritty overrides it, or that `_NET_WM_USER_TIME` needs to be set explicitly after window creation.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Use Cinnamon (or another Mutter-based DE) with `focus-new-windows: strict` and `focus-mode: click`
2. Launch Alacritty from the application menu
3. Observe that the window appears unfocused — keyboard input goes nowhere until you click inside

## Related
- #846 "Takes X keyboard focus upon startup" (opposite issue)
- #2824 "Support for startup notification protocol" (closed, related but different focus)

title: Search across buffers of all windows/tabs (a.k.a. find globally)
author: jlmuir
body: This is a feature request. I'd love it if I could search across the buffers of all windows/tabs. This is my favorite feature of iTerm2. I know I've got a window open somewhere for something, but I can't find it because I have too many windows. With this feature, I can always find that window. iTerm2 calls this feature Find Globally, and it can be accessed with the Command-Shift-F keyboard shortcut or via the "Edit > Find > Find Globally" menu path. There are various ways that a GUI could be implemented for this, but below is what iTerm2's looks like. Clicking on a search result brings that window to the front of the window stack and highlights the search result in the buffer.

title: Copy paste bug in `impl From<SizeInfo<f32>> for SizeInfo<u32>`
author: qarmin
body:
Problem found by AI, validated by me (full report, with more possible bugs - [alacritty_20260627.html](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29850265/alacritty_20260627.html))



### CPY_1 `HIGH`

**Description:** In SizeInfo<u32> From<SizeInfo<f32>> conversion, the 'columns' field is incorrectly set to 'size_info.screen_lines' instead of 'size_info.columns'. This means any code that reads SizeInfo<u32>.columns will silently get the number of screen lines instead, causing wrong damage shapes and potentially incorrect rendering math anywhere SizeInfo<u32> is used.

**Locations:**

```
alacritty/src/display/mod.rs:181
  181 |             columns: size_info.screen_lines,
```

**Fix:** Change 'columns: size_info.screen_lines,' to 'columns: size_info.columns,'

https://github.com/alacritty/alacritty/blob/bdb72b32eeb074e3a0b8559d8ccac458237474a3/alacritty/src/display/mod.rs#L181

title: [Bug] Blur stopped working in KDE Plasma Wayland 6.7
author: OzzyHelix
body: there was an update to how kwin handles request to blur the background and now blur no longer works. it seems to have started to happen in kwin 6.7 and if I revert back to kwin 6.5 it works again so something changed with kwin causing blur to stop working. if you require more information I can provide it on request thanks Ozzy

title: CJK (double-width) characters render as black boxes under tmux until the cell is redrawn
author: cheukswiss
body:
**Description:**

Under tmux, CJK/double-width characters are drawn as solid black boxes when they first appear. As soon as the cell is forced to redraw (scrolling, editing, the cursor passing over it), the character renders correctly. ASCII/latin characters in the *same font* are never affected — only double-width characters. The problem does **not** occur without tmux, and does **not** occur with the same tmux session under Windows Terminal, which points to an interaction between Alacritty's damage tracking and tmux's partial (cell-level) redraws not covering the second cell of a wide character.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Launch Alacritty, start a tmux session.
2. Inside tmux, open a file containing CJK text (e.g. `nvim` on a file with Chinese), or `cat` such a file.
3. Observe: newly-appeared CJK characters show as black boxes.
4. Scroll or move the cursor over them → they render correctly.

**Isolation matrix (already verified):**

| Setup                          | Black boxes? |
| ------------------------------ | ------------ |
| Alacritty + tmux + nvim        | **Yes**      |
| Alacritty + nvim (no tmux)     | No           |
| Windows Terminal + tmux + nvim | No           |

**Already ruled out:** font (reproduces with both *CaskaydiaMono Nerd Font* and *Maple Mono NF CN*, the latter containing CJK glyphs directly — so it is not a font-fallback issue), locale (UTF-8 throughout), and nvim config (reproduces with plain `cat` under tmux too).

**Environment:**
- Alacritty: 0.17 (native Windows build)
- OS: Windows host running WSL2 (Ubuntu 24.04.3, kernel 6.6.87.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2)
- tmux: 3.6a, `TERM=tmux-256color` inside tmux
- Shell launched via `wsl.exe`

title: Bug: TUIs like Claude Code get discombobulated/janky over time
author: jchook
body:
When I use Claude Code for a long time in Alacritty + Linux, the continued rendering gets all janky and discombobulated -- sometimes input jumps to the bottom, sometimes to the top, the TUI rendering get all confused and turns into a huge mess.

It's difficult to reproduce but happens consistently. My theory is full/overflowing scroll buffer + scroll back during TUI output.

I do not see this issue on Terminal.app, Kitty, or Ghostty.

I looked for a similar issue and didn't see one. I understand Alacritty has a very minimal approach -- just kindly reporting my experience as it's an increasingly common workflow and is forcing me to switch to Kitty.

```
❯ alacritty --version
alacritty 0.17.0

❯ xmonad --version
xmonad 0.18.0.9

❯ zsh --version
zsh 5.9 (x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu)

❯ claude --version
2.1.183 (Claude Code)

❯ cat /etc/*release | head -n 1
NAME="Void"

❯ uname -a
Linux north 6.18.26_1 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sun May  3 01:56:02 UTC 2026 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

title: Advertise Tc capability in terminfo file so that it works with tmux out of the box
author: returnDanilo
body:
Alacritty has true color support but it breaks when using tmux.

This is a common problem that people find; here are 4 examples: #109, #6156, #2487, #3354. As far as I can tell, all of them would have been prevented if Tc were included in the terminfo file. Instead, users have to set a tmux option to tell tmux that the underlying term does support true color.

After that, it'd be good to contact the ncurses maintainer to update their database.

title: Memory leak in ui_config::Hint default allocation (~431 bytes) detected via Valgrind
author: piadi-su
body:
### Bug description
A minor memory leak (~431 bytes) was detected using Valgrind during Alacritty startup/configuration loading. It seems related to the `Default` allocation of `alacritty::config::ui_config::Hint` using an `Rc` pointer.

### To Reproduce
Run Alacritty with Valgrind checking for definite/indirect leaks:
`valgrind --leak-check=full --show-leak-kinds=definite,indirect alacritty`

### Actual behavior
Valgrind reports 431 bytes "definitely lost" tracing back to `ui_config.rs`:

```text
==20736== 431 (240 direct, 191 indirect) bytes in 1 blocks are definitely lost in loss record 277 of 427
==20736==    at 0x508A8A8: malloc (vg_replace_malloc.c:446)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: UnknownInlinedFun (library/std/src/sys/alloc/unix.rs:14)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: UnknownInlinedFun (library/std/src/alloc.rs:455)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: alloc (library/alloc/src/alloc.rs:95)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: alloc_impl_runtime (library/alloc/src/alloc.rs:190)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: alloc_impl (library/alloc/src/alloc.rs:312)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: allocate (library/alloc/src/alloc.rs:429)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: exchange_malloc (library/alloc/src/alloc.rs:489)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: new<alloc::rc::RcInner<alacritty::config::ui_config::Hint>> (library/alloc/src/boxed.rs:265)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: new<alacritty::config::ui_config::Hint> (library/alloc/src/rc.rs:420)
==20736==    by 0x441BD5A: <alacritty::config::ui_config::Hints as core::default::Default>::default (ui_config.rs:267)
==20736==    by 0x4429704: default (ui_config.rs:84)
==20736==    by 0x4429704: <alacritty::config::ui_config::UiConfigVisitor as serde_core::de::Visitor>::visit_map (ui_config.rs:43)
==20736==    by 0x427DCA9: <toml::value::Value as serde_core::de::Deserializer>::deserialize_any (src/value.rs:545)
==20736==    by 0x421C6AE: read_config (mod.rs:188)
==20736==    by 0x421C6AE: alacritty::config::load_from (mod.rs:169)
==20736==    by 0x421B40B: alacritty::config::load (mod.rs:134)
==20736==    by 0x42BD2DB: alacritty::main (main.rs:88)

### Environment

OS: arch linux
VERSION: alacritty 0.17.0 (94e7c887)

title: Opacity not working on Windows 11
author: Alex-Velez
body: The `opacity` setting seems to not work on my system. ### System ### `alacritty.toml` (located in `C:\Users\XXX\AppData\Roaming\alacritty`) ```toml [general] live_config_reload = true working_directory = "C:/Users/XXX" # import = ["~/.config/alacritty/themes/themes/rose_pine.toml"] [env] TERM = "alacritty" [terminal] shell = { program = "C:/Users/XXX/AppData/Local/Programs/nu/bin/nu.exe" } [window] dynamic_title = true title = "Alacritty" startup_mode = "Windowed" option_as_alt = "Both" # No effect on Windows/Linux opacity = 0.95 blur = true dynamic_padding = true padding = { x = 10, y = 0 } decorations = "Full" decorations_theme_variant = "Dark" dimensions = { columns = 120, lines = 30} [scrolling] history = 1000 multiplier = 3 [font] normal = { family = "ComicShannsMono Nerd Font Mono", style = "Regular" } size = 12.5 offset = { x = 0, y = 0 } glyph_offset = { x = 0, y = 0 } [colors] draw_bold_text_with_bright_colors = true transparent_background_colors = true primary = { background = "#161616" } [bell] animation = "EaseOutExpo" color = "#C0C5CE" command = "None" duration = 0 [selection] save_to_clipboard = true semantic_escape_chars = ",│`|:\"' ()[]{}<>\t" [cursor] blink_interval = 650 blink_timeout = 5 thickness = 0.25 unfocused_hollow = true style = { shape = "Block", blinking = "On"} vi_mode_style = { shape = "Block", blinking = "Never" } [debug] prefer_egl = true ``` ### `alacritty -vv` output ``` Created log file at "C:\Users\XXX\AppData\Local\Temp\Alacritty-52452.log" [0.000003500s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Welcome to Alacritty [0.000995600s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Version 0.17.0 (94e7c88) [0.005680900s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Configuration files loaded from: "C:\\Users\\XXX\\AppData\\Roaming\\alacritty\\alacritty.toml" [0.037494700s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Window scale factor: 2 [0.376708900s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Using WGL [0.378021400s] [DEBUG] [alacritty] Picked GL Config: buffer_type: Some(Rgb { r_size: 8, g_size: 8, b_size: 8 }) alpha_size: 8 num_samples: 0 hardware_accelerated: true supports_transparency: Some(false) config_api: Api(OPENGL | GLES1 | GLES2) srgb_capable: true [0.396707000s] [DEBUG] [alacritty] Loading "ComicShannsMono Nerd Font Mono" font [0.613053400s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Running on AMD Radeon 780M Graphics [0.613449600s] [INFO ] [alacritty] OpenGL version 3.3.0 Core Profile Context 23.20.60.05.240328, shader_version 4.60 [0.613937400s] [INFO ] [alacritty] GPU reset notifications are disabled [0.614262100s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Using OpenGL 3.3 renderer [0.680731400s] [DEBUG] [alacritty] Enabled debug logging for OpenGL [0.681535000s] [DEBUG] [alacritty] Filling glyph cache with common glyphs [0.723116200s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Cell size: 18 x 40 [0.723500000s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Padding: 20 x 0 [0.723831000s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Width: 2200, Height: 1200 [0.801119300s] [INFO ] [alacritty] PTY dimensions: 30 x 120 [0.804021500s] [INFO ] [alacritty_terminal] Using conpty.dll for pseudoconsole [0.821433700s] [INFO ] [alacritty] Initialisation complete ``` For some reason, I cannot get `supports_transparency: Some(false)` to turn `true`. ### Side note 1 I believe it is unlikely that my drivers have an issue, since I am able to successfully get Windows Terminal to display a transparent background. But, of course, I'll try anything. ### Side note 2 I have tried the solutions from [Issue 7283](https://github.com/alacritty/alacritty/issues/7283), but none seem to work on my system. ### Side note 3 Alacritty was installed using the MSI installer (`.exe` is located at `C:\Program Files\Alacritty\alacritty.exe`), however the issue persists when installing through `cargo install alacritty` Thank you, Alex

title: Dynamic fontconfig font size query not respected
author: leana8959
body:
I have a custom fontconfig xml file that tells programs to use a thicker variant of Iosevka font when font size is small.

<details>

<summary>fontconfig</summary>

```xml
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE fontconfig SYSTEM "urn:fontconfig:fonts.dtd">
<fontconfig>
  <match target="pattern">
    <test name="family" compare="contains"><string>Iosevka</string></test>
    <edit name="weight" mode="assign" binding="strong">
      <if> <eq><name>weight</name><const>bold</const></eq> <!-- then --> <const>bold</const>
        <if> <eq><name>weight</name><const>medium</const></eq> <!-- then --> <const>medium</const>
          <!-- not bold nor medium -->
          <if> <more><name>size</name><double>15</double></more> <!-- then --> <const>regular</const>
            <if> <more><name>size</name><double>10</double></more> <!-- then --> <const>medium</const>
              <!-- else --> <const>bold</const>
            </if>
          </if>
        </if>
      </if>
    </edit>
  </match>
</fontconfig>
```

</details>

I expect alacritty to use a thicker font when the font size is small (<kbd>ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>-</kbd>), and use a thinner font when the font size is big (<kbd>ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>=</kbd>). alacritty only shows the font in bold using the fontconfig above, however ghostty handles this correctly.

I apologize for having such a cursed configuration, but it is very useful and I would like to see alacritty handle this correctly!
